billiard_reference_workflow.contract: Create one active cue/driver body and a passive target rack with stable ids.
billiard_reference_workflow.name: Compile object graph

billiard_reference_workflow.contract: Use sphere or sphere-like colliders with explicit radius/diameter semantics and no t=0 overlap.
billiard_reference_workflow.name: Bind physical assets

billiard_reference_workflow.contract: Driver velocity or impulse encodes requested speed; target initial velocities are zero.
billiard_reference_workflow.name: Set physics controls

billiard_reference_workflow.contract: Runtime, not the LLM, produces target motion through contact events.
billiard_reference_workflow.name: Execute runtime

billiard_reference_workflow.contract: Reject if passive targets move above threshold before first active contact.
billiard_reference_workflow.name: Verify causality

billiard_reference_workflow.contract: Tune speed, restitution, friction, spacing, and solver substeps only after causality passes.
billiard_reference_workflow.name: Iterate controls

capabilities.description: Compatibility alias for billiards-specific cases. Prefer rigid_body_contact_causality for new planners and case templates.

terms=("billiard", "billiards", "pool", "cue ball", "cue_ball", "台球", "白球", "目标球", "ball collision"),
"For billiard-like scenes, active cue/impactor bodies should get non-zero initial_velocity_m_s, passive target balls must start with [0,0,0], "
| billiards causality | `billiard_causality_compiler` | active striker moves first; passive targets move only after runtime contact propagation |
"description": "Compile billiards / pool / cue-ball prompts into active-striker and passive-target rigid-body cases with contact-driven target motion.",
| billiards / pool / cue ball / ball collision | `billiard_causality_compiler` |
plan = self.planner.plan("billiards cue ball hits passive target")

failure_modes: Targets move before contact because of initial velocity leakage.
Targets move before contact because colliders overlap at t=0.
LLM scale is interpreted as radius in one stage and diameter in another.
A visually good render is accepted without trajectory causality evidence.
iteration_moves: Inspect physics.json for passive initial velocities.
Inspect first trajectory frames for target speed before first active contact.
Adjust radius/extent and initial spacing before increasing speed.
Only then sweep driver speed, restitution, and friction to tune spread.
prompt_moves: Express one active striker and a passive target group.
Let the LLM infer reasonable mass, radius, friction, and restitution, but keep passive initial velocity at zero.
Use speed wording such as slow, medium, or fast as control intent, not as a template selection.
runtime_contract: Cue or driver body may have initial velocity or impulse.
Passive target bodies start with zero linear and angular velocity.
Collision geometry must not overlap at t=0; small spheres keep their explicit radius instead of being clamped larger.
Trajectory and contact events are the only source of post-contact target motion.
capabilities.title: Billiards Contact Causality Case Family (Compatibility Alias)
verifier_checks: First active contact exists between driver and target group.
Passive pre-contact speed stays below threshold.
Post-contact target displacement or spread matches requested intensity.
Contact events include the expected driver-target pair and optional secondary contacts.

capabilities.description: Compile domino or chain-reaction prompts into ordered contact propagation where only the first body is actively triggered and later bodies move after upstream contact.

failure_modes: Later chain objects are pre-animated or pre-rotated.
Contact pairs are missing even though objects appear to fall.
Tip order is reversed or simultaneous beyond tolerance.
iteration_moves: Check initial angular velocity on all non-first objects.
Inspect adjacent contact pair coverage.
Adjust spacing, mass, friction, and angular damping after sequence validity passes.
prompt_moves: Define an ordered chain with stable ids.
Allow only the first object to receive active trigger motion.
Require later objects to remain passive until contact propagation reaches them.
runtime_contract: First chain object may have impulse or angular velocity.
Subsequent chain objects start with zero linear and angular velocity.
Trajectory records increasing tip/motion start times and adjacent contact pairs.
capabilities.title: Sequential Contact Propagation
verifier_checks: Non-first objects have no initial motion.
Tip or motion start times are non-decreasing along the chain.
Adjacent contact pairs are present in order.

Motion after render is explained by contact.

capabilities.description: Validate restitution-controlled rigid-body rebound after impact. The smoke family uses a ball dropping onto a support surface, but the invariant applies to any bouncing rigid body.

evidence.text: Validate restitution-controlled rigid-body rebound after impact.

failure_modes: F1_missing_trajectory
F2_missing_contact_events
F3_invalid_initial_physics_state
F4_causality_violation
F7_runtime_artifact_incomplete
iteration_moves: Inspect z trajectory before and after first support contact.
Check restitution label and rebound-height envelope before tuning material.
Reject energy gain unless an external impulse is declared.
prompt_moves: Identify bouncing body, support surface, drop height, restitution, gravity, and expected rebound envelope.
runtime_contract: Bouncing body descends under gravity before first contact.
Impact contact event is exported.
Post-contact rebound height is bounded by restitution and initial drop height.
capabilities.title: Bounce Restitution Validation
verifier_checks: Object descends before first contact.
First impact contact exists.
Post-contact rebound height is not too low for restitution.
Post-contact rebound height does not exceed the allowed energy envelope.

capabilities.description: Validate friction-controlled rolling motion on a flat support surface. The smoke family uses a ball with initial horizontal velocity, but the invariant applies to any rolling rigid body with contact friction.

evidence.text: Validate friction-controlled rolling motion on a flat support surface.

failure_modes: F1_missing_trajectory
F2_missing_contact_events
F3_invalid_initial_physics_state
F4_causality_violation
F7_runtime_artifact_incomplete
iteration_moves: Inspect support contact events before interpreting rolling distance.
Compare initial and final horizontal speed against friction labels.
Reject too-long travel or too-short travel before tuning material properties.
prompt_moves: Identify rolling body, support surface, initial horizontal velocity, friction, and expected distance envelope.
runtime_contract: Rolling body starts with declared horizontal velocity.
Support contact is exported during rolling.
Horizontal speed decays and travel distance remains friction-bounded.
capabilities.title: Rolling Friction Validation
verifier_checks: Initial horizontal velocity is non-zero.
Support contact exists during rolling.
Horizontal speed decays.
Forward displacement is within the expected friction response envelope.

capabilities.description: Validate flat-surface sliding friction and static-friction threshold behavior. The smoke family uses a crate, but the invariant applies to any sliding rigid body with a support contact and friction material.

evidence.text: Validate flat-surface sliding friction and static-friction threshold behavior.

failure_modes: F1_missing_trajectory
F2_missing_contact_events
F3_invalid_initial_physics_state
F4_causality_violation
F7_runtime_artifact_incomplete
iteration_moves: Inspect support contact before checking sliding distance.
Compare dynamic-friction stopping distance against final speed.
For static-threshold cases, verify applied force is below static limit before expecting no motion.
prompt_moves: Identify sliding body, support surface, initial horizontal velocity or applied force, static friction, dynamic friction, and expected stop/static envelope.
runtime_contract: Sliding mode starts with declared horizontal velocity and decelerates.
Static-threshold mode declares applied force below static friction limit and remains nearly stationary.
Support contact and friction labels are exported.
capabilities.title: Sliding Crate Friction Validation
verifier_checks: Support contact exists.
Sliding body decelerates and stops within distance range.
Static-threshold body remains under displacement/speed thresholds.
Motion does not accelerate or reverse without declared external force.

iteration_playbook.input: README/docs, tests, runtime code, and public config
iteration_playbook.rule: Use WHAT/HOW/FLOW/BRIDGE extraction; do not publish raw sessions.
Remove private paths, secrets, and raw logs; keep owner files and checks.
Run several prompts and classify failures by stage before changing code.

iteration_playbook.output: stage-specific prompt, asset, runtime, verifier, or benchmark patch
iteration_playbook.rule: Change one responsible stage at a time and rerun tests.
